warnings.message: seasonId 'season-1' has no corresponding season object in the world's 'seasons' array — the world only lists a display string 'Season 1: First Year', not an id. This works today but is a fragile implicit mapping if seasons are ever formalized as objects.
warnings.suggestion: Either add an explicit id/name mapping for seasons in the world schema, or document that seasonId is a free-form slug not directly tied to the world.seasons string array.

warnings.message: branchDialogue coverage is incomplete for some combinatorial paths. For example, a player who chooses choice-2:a (popular path) and then choice-5:a (integrated ending) lands on scene-ending-integrated, but branchDialogue only supplies variants keyed 'branch-integrated', 'branch-authentic', and 'branch-bridge' for that scene — there is no 'branch-popular' variant, so that specific combo would presumably fall back to a generic/default variant (branch-integrated) with no acknowledgment of the earlier popular-table choice.
warnings.suggestion: Either add a branch-popular variant for scene-ending-integrated (and equivalent missing combos for the other two endings) or confirm/document that the base 'ending' branchId (e.g. branch-integrated) is always used as a fallback when the earlier-path branch has no specific line, so silent path combos degrade gracefully.

warnings.message: estimatedPlayTime is listed as 12 minutes, but the episode contains ~24 scenes and 7 choice points with fairly dense dialogue; actual read-through time for a careful player is likely higher.
warnings.suggestion: Re-time a full playthrough (including the longest branch, e.g. Front Steps → Hallway → Classroom → Lunch Setup → Lunch-Alex → Alex-Confront → Project Team → Climax → Ending) and adjust estimatedPlayTime accordingly, or note that this reflects a single-branch estimate rather than full content.

warnings.message: Mr. Alvarez's character sheet mentions a running classroom whiteboard gag ('DAYS SINCE INCIDENT') and a bartender mug, neither of which is referenced anywhere in the episode's actual scene descriptions or dialogue.
warnings.suggestion: Not required for this episode, but consider a small visual beat referencing these established character details to reinforce continuity/characterization if the scene is ever expanded.

recommendations: Consider adding explicit 'startScene' metadata at the episode level (currently inferred from array order = scene-arrival) to remove ambiguity for the rendering pipeline.
Consider filling in the missing combinatorial branchDialogue variants (or explicitly documenting fallback behavior) so no player-path combination reaches an ending with only generic narration.
Double-check estimated play time against a full manual playthrough timing pass before publishing.
